name: Canadian French

formality.description: Canadian French follows the same T-V system as France French but Québécois usage trends more toward tu-form in everyday contexts, including some software. The vocabulary differs significantly from France French (e.g., 'courriel' not 'e-mail', 'magasiner' not 'faire du shopping').

gender.inclusiveGuidance: Quebec has been at the forefront of écriture inclusive in French. The Office québécois de la langue française promotes gender-inclusive writing. Doublet forms ('les utilisateurs et les utilisatrices') or truncated forms ('les utilisateur·rice·s') are both accepted.

professional-vous.label: Vouvoiement (Professional)
professional-vous.description: Standard for software targeting Quebec and Canadian French speakers.
professional-vous.prompt: Professional Canadian French (Québécois). Use vous-form. Québécois vocabulary — use 'courriel' not 'e-mail', 'télécharger' not 'downloader'.

casual-tu.label: Tutoiement (Casual)
casual-tu.description: For consumer apps and casual Québécois contexts.
casual-tu.prompt: Casual Canadian French. Use tu-form. Québécois vocabulary and tone. Natural conversational style.

notes: Québécois French has significant vocabulary and idiomatic differences from France French.
